description: Features selected by full-dataset correlation overfit selection and fail to generalize.
body: Failure mode: a feature subset is chosen by ranking correlations on the entire dataset and keeping the top-k, so the selection overfits noise and may include leaky or test-contaminated signals that do not hold on new data. Detection signals: a selected set that changes drastically between cross-validation folds, and a holdout metric well below the in-sample selection metric. Do not freeze the unstable subset for production. Safer fallback: compute importance and stability across folds, keep only features stable across folds, exclude any leaky candidates, and confirm the pruned set preserves metric on a fresh holdout.
